title: Paul Bunyan and Babe the Blue Ox

q.en: How tall was Paul Bunyan, according to the tale?
q.zh: 根据故事，保罗·班扬有多高？

options.en: About six feet tall
options.zh: 大约六英尺高

options.en: As tall as a tree exactly according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 和一棵树一样高（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Ten axe handles high
options.zh: 十把斧柄那么高

options.en: Sixty-three axe handles high
options.zh: 六十三把斧柄那么高

explain.en: The tale says Paul was a giant lumberjack who 'stood sixty-three axe handles high, give or take a handle.'
explain.zh: 故事说保罗是个巨人伐木工，“身高六十三把斧柄，上下差不了一把”。

q.en: How did Paul meet Babe the Blue Ox?
q.zh: 保罗是怎么遇到蓝牛贝贝的？

options.en: Babe was born at the logging camp according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 贝贝出生在伐木营（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He bought him at a store
options.zh: 他在商店买的

options.en: He won him in a contest
options.zh: 他在比赛中赢来的

options.en: He found him as a blue baby ox during the Winter of the Blue Snow
options.zh: 在“蓝雪之冬”时，他发现了一头蓝色的牛宝宝

explain.en: During the Winter of the Blue Snow, Paul found a snowdrift with two blue ears; inside was a sickly baby ox turned blue from the cold, whom he named Babe.
explain.zh: 在“蓝雪之冬”，保罗发现一个雪堆露出两只蓝耳朵，里面是一头被冻得发蓝、虚弱的牛宝宝，他给它起名贝贝。

q.en: Why did Paul use his axe to dig the Great Lakes?
q.zh: 保罗为什么用斧头挖出了五大湖？

options.en: To make a place for the lumberjacks to fish according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 给伐木工弄个钓鱼的地方（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To hide from danger
options.zh: 躲避危险

options.en: Because no local watering hole was big enough for Babe to drink from
options.zh: 因为当地没有够大的水坑让贝贝喝水

options.en: To build a baseball field
options.zh: 建一个棒球场

explain.en: Babe grew so large that none of the local watering holes were big enough for him to drink from, so Paul solved the problem by digging the Great Lakes with his axe.
explain.zh: 贝贝长得太大，当地的水坑都不够它喝，于是保罗用斧头挖出了五大湖来解决这个问题。

q.en: How did Paul solve the problem of not enough pancakes for his workers?
q.zh: 保罗是怎样解决工人 pancakes（煎饼）不够吃的问题的？

options.en: He ordered pizza instead
options.zh: 他改叫披萨

options.en: He had a mile-wide iron griddle built, and Babe drank a lake for the batter bowl
options.zh: 他造了一块一英里宽的铁煎盘，还让贝贝喝干一个湖当面糊碗

options.en: He told the cooks to work faster according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他叫厨子干快点（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He sent the workers home
options.zh: 他把工人打发回家

explain.en: Paul had a mile-wide iron griddle built in the big city; Babe drank all the water out of a lake so the cooks could use the empty lake bed as a bowl to mix batter.
explain.zh: 保罗在大城市造了一块一英里宽的铁煎盘；贝贝喝干了一个湖的水，厨子们就把干涸的湖床当碗来调面糊。

q.en: What makes this story a folk tale, or 'tall tale'?
q.zh: 是什么让这个故事成为民间传说，也就是“夸张故事（tall tale）”？

options.en: It uses exaggeration (like a mile-wide griddle) and explains how natural features formed
options.zh: 它用了夸张（如一英里宽的煎盘），还解释了自然景物是怎么形成的

options.en: It teaches math facts
options.zh: 它教数学常识

options.en: It is a science experiment
options.zh: 它是一项科学实验

options.en: It is a true news report according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它是一篇真实的新闻报道（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Tall tales use wild exaggeration and often explain how real places formed; here Paul 'digging the Great Lakes' and 'straightening a river' are examples.
explain.zh: 夸张故事用极度夸张的手法，并常解释真实地貌的由来；本书中保罗“挖出五大湖”“把河拉直”就是例子。

q.en: Which detail shows this is a tall tale rather than a real event?
q.zh: 哪个细节说明这是个夸张故事，而不是真实事件？

options.en: Babe liked pancakes
options.zh: 贝贝喜欢煎饼

options.en: Paul had parents
options.zh: 保罗有父母

options.en: Impossible, exaggerated events, such as digging the Great Lakes with an axe
options.zh: 不可能发生的夸张情节，比如用斧头挖出五大湖

options.en: Paul worked at a logging camp according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 保罗在伐木营工作过（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Events like a man digging the Great Lakes with an axe or a mile-wide griddle are impossible, which is the hallmark of a tall tale.
explain.zh: 像一个人用斧头挖出五大湖、造出一英里宽的煎盘这种事不可能发生，正是夸张故事的标志。

q.en: The tale explains how certain real places were made. Which ones?
q.zh: 这个故事解释了某些真实地点的由来。是哪些？

options.en: The Great Lakes and the straightened Wisconsin river
options.zh: 五大湖和被拉直的威斯康星河

options.en: The Rocky Mountains and the Mississippi River according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 落基山脉和密西西比河（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The Pacific Ocean and the Sahara Desert
options.zh: 太平洋和撒哈拉沙漠

options.en: The Grand Canyon and Niagara Falls
options.zh: 大峡谷和尼亚加拉瀑布

explain.en: The story says Paul dug the Great Lakes for Babe to drink, and later used a giant chain to pull and straighten a twisty river in Wisconsin.
explain.zh: 故事说保罗为让贝贝喝水挖出了五大湖，后来又用巨链把威斯康星一条弯弯的河拉直了。

q.en: Paul uses his axe to dig lakes and a giant chain to straighten a river. What does this show about him?
q.zh: 保罗用斧头挖湖，用巨链把河拉直。这说明了他怎样的性格？

options.en: He was clever and used tools to solve problems
options.zh: 他很聪明，会借助工具解决问题

options.en: He was lazy
options.zh: 他很懒惰

options.en: He gave up easily
options.zh: 他轻易放弃

options.en: He was afraid of water according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他怕水（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Each time Paul faces a problem (no water for Babe, a crooked river jamming logs), he invents a clever tool-based solution, showing his resourcefulness.
explain.zh: 每次遇到问题（贝贝没水喝、弯河堵住原木），保罗都想出巧妙的、借助工具的解决办法，显出他的机智。

q.en: Why did the author retell this folk tale?
q.zh: 作者为什么重述这个民间传说？

options.en: To teach a foreign language
options.zh: 教一门外语

options.en: To entertain readers with a fun legend and humorously explain natural features
options.zh: 用有趣的传说娱乐读者，并以幽默方式解释自然景物

options.en: To give a science lesson about lumber according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 上一堂关于木材的科学课（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To report real news
options.zh: 报道真实新闻

explain.en: As a retold folk tale, the author's purpose is to entertain with a larger-than-life American legend while playfully explaining how landmarks came to be.
explain.zh: 作为重述的民间传说，作者的目的是用夸张的美国传奇娱乐读者，同时俏皮地解释地标的由来。

q.en: What view of America's past does this tale celebrate?
q.zh: 这个故事赞美了美国过去的哪一面？

options.en: Modern city life
options.zh: 现代城市生活

options.en: Early pioneers, lumberjacks, and the wild wilderness
options.zh: 早期的拓荒者、伐木工和荒野

options.en: The invention of computers according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 电脑的发明（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Life in outer space
options.zh: 外太空生活

explain.en: The tale celebrates the lumberjack era of early America, when giants like Paul cleared forests and tamed the wilderness as the country grew.
explain.zh: 故事赞美了早期美国的伐木时代：像保罗这样的巨人砍伐森林、驯服荒野，伴随国家一同成长。

prompt.en: Tall tales like Paul Bunyan use wild exaggeration to explain how things in nature came to be. If you made up your own tall tale, what natural feature would your hero create, and how?
prompt.zh: 像保罗·班扬这样的夸张故事用极度夸张来解释自然事物是怎么形成的。如果你编一个自己的夸张故事，你的主人公会造出什么自然景物，又是怎么造的？

guidance.en:
• Name a real natural feature (like a lake, mountain, or river) your hero would create.
• Use funny exaggeration, the way Paul 'dug the Great Lakes' or 'straightened a river.'
• Show how the hero's size, strength, or a silly accident caused the feature to form.
guidance.zh:
• 说出你的主人公会创造的真实自然景物（如湖、山或河）。
• 用上搞笑的夸张，就像保罗“挖出五大湖”或“把河拉直”那样。
• 说明主人公的体型、力气或某个滑稽的意外如何造成了这个景物。
